generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLidar : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST10LX LiDAR providing distance measurements"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudTransmission
]

instance IntelDeepSenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel DeepSense D435i depth camera providing RGBD data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RGBDImageTransmission
]

instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vedder Electronic Speed Controller reporting motor state feedback"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStateTransmission
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLidar
]

instance RGBDImageTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepSenseD435iCamera
]

instance MotorStateTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VedderElectronicSpeedController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data stream"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler, DataStorageEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudTransmission
]

instance RGBDImageData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw RGBD image data stream from depth camera"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler, DataStorageEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData RGBDImageTransmission
]

instance MotorStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor state feedback data including speed and torque"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStateTransmission
]

instance ProcessedPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Clustering and tracking processed point cloud data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler, ComputationOffloadEnabler
]

instance ProcessedRGBDImageData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed RGBD image data for depth perception"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler, ComputationOffloadEnabler
]

instance LeadVehicleInfoData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Detected lead vehicle information including speed and position"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadEnabler
]

instance ACCSpeedCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Acceleration command computed by ACC algorithm to be sent to vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadEnabler
]

// Enablers
instance ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 middleware for real‑time data exchange between vehicle and twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data processing enabler for clustering, tracking, and data transformation"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data storage enabler persisting sensor data for later analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ComputationOffloadEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Computation offloading enabler for cloud‑based ACC algorithm"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

// Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time monitoring of vehicle state, projected path, and nearby objects provides reports"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualMonitoringInsight, ReportGenerationAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of ACC algorithm on the twin computes speed command for vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCSpeedCommandAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote control of ACC, desired speed, and emergency braking via user interface"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

// Insights / Actions
instance VisualMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time 3D visualization of vehicle state and sensor data"
]

instance ReportGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Allows user to generate and save reports of observed vehicle data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ACCSpeedCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Speed command output by ACC algorithm for vehicle actuation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enable or disable Adaptive Cruise Control via user interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Set desired cruising speed through remote management"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issue emergency braking command in critical situations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands generated by the Digital Twin—such as the computed desired speed for the ACC algorithm, ACC enable/disable commands, and emergency‑brake requests—are transmitted back to the physical twin over the same Wi‑Fi UDP link. The commands are consumed by the Actuation FE on the physical side, which translates the desired speed into motor voltage commands and triggers actuation of steering or braking. The documents do not specify explicit validation, authentication, or acknowledgment mechanisms for these command messages."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twin operates with a shared time reference required for computation offloading, enforcing a clock synchronization accuracy of 100 ms across devices; although safety‑critical vehicular standards mandate stricter synchronization, the system limits to 100 ms because of hardware constraints."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall constellation follows the ISO 23247 reference architecture, mapping each functional entity to a ROS2 package that runs on the designated hardware, thereby aligning the Physical Twin Entity (sensor/actuator and preprocessing services), the Digital Twin Entity (data management and control services), and the User Entity (3D visualization and control‑panel services) within an entity‑based modular structure."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DTS internal software components communicate via a ROS 2 network (publish/subscribe model). Communication between the Physical Twin Entity and the Digital Twin Entity occurs over a Wi‑Fi network using UDP at the transport layer. The documents do not provide quantitative latency, bandwidth, reliability, redundancy, or security specifications."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The computational part of the DT that runs the ACC algorithm is off‑loaded from the resource‑constrained ECU to external compute resources described as “in the cloud”. The DT therefore is deployed on cloud infrastructure that provides sufficient processing and storage for data collection, reporting and remote management. Details on edge or on‑premise deployment, scalability mechanisms or availability guarantees are not provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system is explicitly based on the ISO 23247 standard for manufacturing digital twins. ISO 23247 defines four parts: (1) overview and general principles, (2) reference architecture (adopted in the design), (3) digital representation of physical manufacturing elements, and (4) information exchange. The architecture adopts the ISO 23247 entity model—Observable Manufacturing Element, Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity—while introducing a Physical Twin Entity to host compute, sensor, and actuator resources. Communication and interoperability are implemented using ROS 2 packages and nodes, not the OPC UA example mentioned in the background. Timing synchronization for distributed computation off‑loading requires a clock accuracy of 100 ms, stated as a hardware limitation, even though many safety‑critical vehicular standards demand tighter synchronization. No additional certification or industry‑specific compliance frameworks are reported in the documents."
]
